title: StudioDash FAQ
description: Answers to common StudioDash questions about availability, platforms, integrations, analytics, reporting, sync, security, and roadmap items.
summary: Common questions about StudioDash, its private-beta status, target platforms, documented integrations, provider data, and roadmap language.

sections.heading: Quick answers
paragraphs: This support section summarises the main public StudioDash questions for developers reviewing the product before private beta. StudioDash is still in development, so the answers distinguish current guide, planned beta access, pre-release integrations, and roadmap features.
For the most complete flow through the guide, start with the overview, then read Integrations, Dashboard, Revenue Analytics, Reporting, Automation & Sync, and Security & Privacy.

sections.heading: How to use this FAQ
paragraphs: Use these answers to understand the product direction and public terminology. They are not a replacement for provider guide, App Store Connect account guidance, Cloudflare token guidance, Google OAuth consent guidance, or final release notes.

When an answer mentions planned, pre-release, roadmap, or private beta behavior, treat that wording as intentional. It means the public website is documenting direction without claiming every feature is generally available.

faq.question: What is StudioDash?
faq.answer: StudioDash is a Mac and iPad dashboard for indie app and game developers. It is designed to bring revenue, app status, website analytics, provider health, sync status, and trends into one focused workspace.

faq.question: Who is StudioDash for?
faq.answer: StudioDash is designed for solo developers, small studios, and creators who manage apps, games, product websites, advertising, and provider dashboards.

faq.question: Is StudioDash available now?
faq.answer: StudioDash is currently marked as Private Beta Planned on the BarkinMad Studios website. A limited number of independent developers and small studios will be invited before public release.

faq.question: Which platforms are planned?
faq.answer: StudioDash is documented as a native Mac and iPad dashboard.

faq.question: Which integrations are documented?
faq.answer: Current public guide covers App Store Connect, AdMob, AdSense, Cloudflare, Google Search Console, Google Ads pre-release coverage, GitHub pre-release coverage, and related provider-health language.

faq.question: Are all documented integrations live?
faq.answer: No. StudioDash guide separates implemented coverage from pre-release and roadmap items. Pre-release integrations should not be read as live provider reporting.

faq.question: Does StudioDash replace provider dashboards?
faq.answer: No. StudioDash is intended to provide a unified operating view for repeated checks. Provider dashboards remain the authority for account administration, billing, permissions, advanced provider-specific setup, and deeper management.

faq.question: What does provider health mean?
faq.answer: Provider health explains whether a connected service looks usable, needs attention, has an error, is not configured, or is planned. It helps users understand whether a missing metric is a product issue, a provider setup issue, or simply unavailable data.

faq.question: What is the difference between demo, pre-release, and live data?
faq.answer: Demo data is sample data used to explain the product experience. Pre-release coverage represents a provider or feature area that is documented but not live reporting. Live data comes from configured provider access and should be clearly separated from both demo and pre-release content.

faq.question: How does StudioDash handle reporting periods?
faq.answer: StudioDash guide describes selected-period summaries for metrics such as revenue, downloads, website traffic, and search visibility where provider history is available. Current operational states such as provider health and app lifecycle are latest-state signals rather than historical totals.

faq.question: Are exports and scheduled reports available?
faq.answer: CSV export, PDF export, scheduled reports, forecasting, and AI summaries should be treated as roadmap or tier-linked product direction unless a public release confirms availability.

faq.question: How should I think about security and privacy?
faq.answer: StudioDash is designed around provider-specific access, protected credential handling, local operational data, and clear separation between demo, pre-release, and live information. Users should configure provider permissions according to their own account policies.

faq.question: Where should I start in the guide?
faq.answer: Start with the StudioDash overview, then review Features, Integrations, Dashboard, Revenue Analytics, Reporting, Automation & Sync, Security & Privacy, and the Screenshots page.
